LEVEL_UP_LINES: A damp little certificate slid under the door. It has your name on it.
The walls moaned your username. Nobody liked that.
Echo checked your file and whispered, "Oh no, this one has stamina."
A nearby toaster called you a freak and then blushed.
The Place has upgraded you from mistake to recurring problem.
Someone in HR just added a new policy because of you.
Your aura is now sticky enough to be detected by sensors.
Echo has marked you as handled with gloves only.
A clipboard appeared with your name under "Do Not Leave Unsupervised."
The carpet remembers what you did. The carpet is seeking therapy.
You have achieved suspiciously moist regional status.
The walls are no longer listening. They are taking notes.
A vending machine blinked twice and offered you hush money.
Echo sighed, lit a cigarette, and updated your threat level.
Your community presence now requires a wet floor sign.
A nurse somewhere just felt a disturbance in the waiting room.
The Place filed you under "technically legal, spiritually troubling."
Someone whispered "throat goat" and the lights flickered in approval.
Your gag reflex stat increased. The server pretended not to notice.
A bedframe warranty has been voided in your honour.
Echo has issued you a provisional hoe licence.
The council met for 3 hours and still could not classify you.
Your name has been added to the emergency contact regret list.
A suspiciously damp plaque appeared. It is warm.
The server gained 3% humidity when you levelled up.
Echo now refers to you internally as "the incident."
You are no longer background noise. You are foreground filth.
A health pamphlet spawned nearby and immediately gave up.
The Place has promoted you from civilian to biological side quest.
A group chat screenshot just became evidence.
Your reputation now requires two-factor contraception.
Echo added a safe word to the patch notes.
The walls clapped. Slowly. Uncomfortably.
A clipboard appeared with every box already ticked, somehow.
You have become load-bearing to the local degeneracy economy.
Somewhere, a consent form printed itself in fear.
Echo looked at your progress and whispered, "professionally ran through."
Your level up caused a minor HR evacuation.
The Place has officially stopped asking questions.
Congratulations. You are now somebody’s reason for therapy.
Echo updated your file from "problem" to "case study."
A mattress in another suburb just filed a complaint.
You have unlocked advanced goblin behaviour.
Your presence has been detected by the Sexual Health Clinic loyalty system.
The vending machine offered you a cigarette and legal advice.
A nearby door unlocked. Not for you. It was escaping.
The Echo Reserve has declined your moral credit application.
Your aura now violates community guidelines in three countries.
The Place has acknowledged your continued descent.
Someone just renamed the server rules channel because of you.
You have become statistically harder to explain.
Echo muttered "absolutely fucking not" and gave you XP anyway.
The lights dimmed out of respect and mild disgust.
A warning label appeared, but it was too late.
You are now considered an environmental hazard after dark.
The community has been forced to perceive you again.
A filing cabinet somewhere made room for your allegations.
Your name is now written in the weird little book.
The static briefly sounded horny, then ashamed.
Echo upgraded you from "recurring character" to "active liability."
A government department almost got involved, then decided it was funnier not to.
Your footsteps now leave emotional damage.
A suspicious puddle formed the shape of your initials.
The server briefly considered celibacy.
You have unlocked forbidden mouth-based reputation scaling.
Echo has added you to the list marked "Do Not Nerf, Just Fear."
A couch somewhere became sticky in your honour.
The Place now recognises you by scent, unfortunately.
Your level up triggered a small but legally distinct moan.
The carpet knows your sins and has chosen silence.
You have been promoted to a Probationary Senior Freak.
The ceiling tiles shifted into a respectful pattern of concern.
Echo tried to bless you, but the blessing came back pregnant.
A little bell rang in a room nobody admits exists.
You have become a workplace incident with legs.
The Place has issued you a ceremonial wet wipe.
A health inspector spawned, looked at you, and logged out.
Your name has been added to the consent form appendix.
The server is now 4% more morally flammable.
Echo nodded once, then immediately denied doing so.

scout.text: You notice a brief staff overlap during shift change. Confusing, but busy.
A side entrance might be in a camera blind spot — hard to tell from here.
A patrol rolls by. Normal presence, nothing aggressive yet.
A delivery truck blocks a side view of the entrance for a moment.
An employee takes a smoke break near the side door.
A camera does a slow motorized sweep across the lobby windows.
You hear faint radio chatter from inside — security is alert but not alarmed.
Construction scaffolding provides an alternate sightline to a side corridor.

entry.text: A couple of late customers linger in the lobby.
A metal detector at the threshold beeps softly when someone passes.
A keypad guards a staff-only doorway.

choices.label: Wait for someone to enter, then slip in

entry.text: A security guard glances your way near the entrance.

choices.label: Hold back and let them lose interest

entry.text: You spot an employee badge left on a counter near a side hallway.

choices.label: Use it immediately to access a door

choices.label: Use the cover and move slowly
Move the cart to create a path

entry.text: You find an alarm panel near the staff entrance.

inside.text: You locate the security room controlling internal cameras.
Footsteps echo nearby — a guard doing rounds.
Three cameras overlap in a corridor — a nasty triangle of coverage.

choices.label: Time the sweep and slip through

inside.text: A civilian steps out of a hallway unexpectedly.
A room of small lockboxes is within reach.
A message pings: an inside contact offers a shortcut… for a cut.
The back office has a safe and employee records on the desk.
A floor manager appears, confused and suspicious.

vault.text: The vault is on a time lock.
A dual-auth lock demands two keys or codes.

choices.label: Search quietly for the second code

vault.text: A thermal drill will work, but the noise will carry.
A fail-safe can flood the vault corridor with gas if tampered with.
You reach the camera core near the vault corridor.
A rapid response unit is moving internally — they’re closing in.
You’re hitting the limit of what you can carry out cleanly.

choices.label: Take only what you can hide

loot.text: You could grab more before leaving.
One bag is heavy and awkward.
You find a stash: easy cash or slower-to-move bonds.
Some bundles might have dye packs. Hard to tell at a glance.
A side drawer in the vault has small valuables and keys.
Employee lockers sit unattended — quick gains, but messy.
You hear a distant alarm clock — someone might be checking the back office soon.

choices.label: Go loud and control the area

loot.text: You spot a hidden compartment behind a loose panel. It could be big… or nothing.

escape.text: Your planned getaway car is visible — but so are a few cameras.

choices.label: Walk away first, then circle back
Ditch the car plan; go on foot

escape.text: A temporary checkpoint is forming two streets away.
A witness is filming on their phone.
The alarm is blaring now — everything feels louder.
A rooftop access door is unlocked. It’s a clean line — if you’re lucky.
You can ditch tools and bags to run lighter — but it costs money.

cleanUp.text: You duck into cover and have a moment.
You still have minor evidence on you.
A contact offers to scrub security footage — but it takes time and focus.
You reach a safehouse. Waiting longer reduces attention, but eats time.
You can swap vehicles and plates, but it’s risky and time-consuming.
You can wash clothes and gloves to reduce traces.
A bag could be ditched — it lowers risk but costs money.

openings: Yeah? Who's this and why are you calling from a masked number?
You've got thirty seconds before I hang up. Go.
Let me guess — urgent account issue? Heard this one before.
I'm in the middle of something. This had better be good.
Unknown caller, weird timing. Start talking.
hangups: You hang up before the pitch lands. They probably knew exactly what you were doing anyway.
You bail early. Sensible — that one sounded like they'd trace the call for sport.

authority: 'Authority card, really?' they mutter. 'Spell the company name for me.'
'Cool. And your staff ID?' The question lands like a trap.
They don't sound impressed. 'Which department? Be specific.'
urgency: 'Urgency is usually where the scam starts,' they reply flatly.
'If it's critical, email me from the official domain.'
They snort. 'Threatening me into compliance? Amateur hour.'
empathy: They let you speak, but only just. 'Mhm. Go on then.'
'You're laying it on a bit thick,' they say, half amused.
They pause. 'Still not convinced, but continue.'
refund: 'A refund?' That at least gets their attention. 'For what exactly?'
They finally sound curious. 'Okay… explain the refund part.'
'Money back?' They stop trying to end the call for a second.
tech: They correct one of your technical terms — annoyingly, but not dismissively.
'That's not how that works,' they say… then ask a follow-up anyway.
You hit closer to something believable. 'Right… continue,' they say.
bank: 'Banks don't do this over the phone,' they cut in immediately.
'No bank rep says it like that.' They're onto the script.
The bank angle lands badly. You can hear them losing patience.
fear: 'If there was fraud, you'd have verified me first.' Ouch.
They don't bite. 'Fear isn't a substitute for proof.'
You push too hard and they push right back.
verification: 'Interesting. And now you want my details? Absolutely not.'
The request lands with a thud. 'Yeah, that's not happening.'
They go colder. 'You're asking the wrong person for that.'
calm: You keep it measured. They don't trust you, but they stop interrupting.
The quieter approach works better. 'Fine… keep going.'
They sound less combative. Not friendly — just curious enough.
compliance: 'Or what?' they ask, clearly enjoying your discomfort.
The pressure lands badly. 'No, you comply.'
'You don't get to order me around,' they reply.
remote: 'Remote tools? On a cold call? Absolutely cooked.'
money: 'Convenient that money's involved only after you called me.'
You mention money too directly. They instantly stiffen.
The pitch smells too cash-focused and they notice.
positive: Against all odds, they don't hang up.
You catch a small opening and wedge the script into it.
They still sound sceptical — but they're listening.
negative: That line lands badly and the silence gets sharp.
You can practically hear them reaching for the block button.
backfire: 'Cute.' They start asking you the sort of questions you can't answer.
The line goes icy. You pushed the wrong button.
That backfires hard. They sound one click away from exposing you.
goSuccess: You push for the details — somehow, they bite.
Against every sane probability, they hand it over.
goFail: They let you finish, then calmly inform you the call is being reported.
'Mate, I work in cyber security.' The line goes dead.

successReplies: They hand over enough to make the call worth it. Barely.
The details come through and you move before they think twice.
declineNotes: A rare moment of restraint. The call centre manager calls it 'wasted potential'.

openings: Hello? Sorry, who am I speaking with?
I don't normally answer unknown numbers, but alright.
Hey, is there a problem with my account or something?
Can you just tell me what this is regarding?
hangups: You drop the call before committing. Not noble — just indecisive.
You hang up. Somewhere, a headset-wearing supervisor sighs theatrically.

authority: 'Okay… and what company are you with exactly?'

dialogueOptions.label: Claim to be Microsoft support
dialogueOptions.line: This is Microsoft support calling about unusual activity on your device.

dialogueOptions.label: Warn of suspicious bank activity
dialogueOptions.line: We've detected suspicious activity against your bank account and need to secure it.

dialogueOptions.label: Threaten account suspension
dialogueOptions.line: If this isn't handled promptly, your account may be suspended for protection.

dialogueOptions.label: Offer a fake refund
dialogueOptions.line: It looks like you're owed a refund — I can process it right now.

dialogueOptions.label: Mention a security issue
dialogueOptions.line: I'm calling regarding a serious security issue tied to your profile.

dialogueOptions.label: Play the helpful expert
dialogueOptions.line: I'll walk you through this step by step so nothing gets missed.

dialogueOptions.label: Quote a compliance requirement
dialogueOptions.line: For compliance reasons I need to verify a couple of details with you now.

dialogueOptions.label: Ask them to open a remote support tool
dialogueOptions.line: I'll need you to open a secure support tool so I can stop the intrusion from my end.

dialogueOptions.label: Request a verification code
dialogueOptions.line: A one-time verification code will be sent — read it back so I can lock everything down.

dialogueOptions.label: Ask them to confirm card details
dialogueOptions.line: To make sure the correct account is secured, please confirm the card you're using.

dialogueOptions.label: Keep it calm and reassuring
dialogueOptions.line: No need to panic — this is exactly why I'm calling before it gets worse.

dialogueOptions.label: Pretend to be the fraud team
dialogueOptions.line: I'm with the fraud response team and your file was escalated to us moments ago.

dialogueOptions.label: Mention an unauthorised login
dialogueOptions.line: We've identified an unauthorised login session and I'm trying to block it before it settles.

dialogueOptions.label: Mention a mistaken chargeback
dialogueOptions.line: There's a mistaken chargeback attached to your file and I can reverse it for you.

dialogueOptions.label: Promise account protection
dialogueOptions.line: I'm trying to make sure you're covered before any losses leave the temporary protection window.

dialogueOptions.label: Use a warm, patient tone
dialogueOptions.line: Take your time — I'll stay on the line and make sure this gets sorted with you.

dialogueOptions.label: Say the window is closing
dialogueOptions.line: I'm trying to stop this before the security window closes in the next few minutes.

dialogueOptions.label: Sound extra formal
dialogueOptions.line: Before I proceed, I am required to complete an immediate account integrity confirmation.

dialogueOptions.label: Build rapport first
dialogueOptions.line: I know this is out of the blue, but I'm calling to help before this becomes a bigger headache for you.

dialogueOptions.label: Mention a payment hold
dialogueOptions.line: A pending hold has been placed against your account activity and I can release it once we verify you.

dialogueOptions.label: Reassure them, then direct them
dialogueOptions.line: You're fine for the moment, but I need you to follow my instructions exactly so it stays that way.

dialogueOptions.label: Bluff an escalation
dialogueOptions.line: If you don't want this escalated further, I can finalise it with you right now.

dialogueOptions.label: Offer to remove a device threat
dialogueOptions.line: There's a flagged process on your device and I can help remove it before it spreads.

dialogueOptions.label: Sound like a calm banker
dialogueOptions.line: I'm just trying to make sure your funds remain secure while this is reviewed.

rareEvents.text: ☎️ **Call Centre Supervisor:** A hiss in your headset tells you to sound more confident. Somehow, it helps.
☎️ **Victim Calls You Out:** They go quiet for a second. You just lost momentum.
☎️ **Scam Jackpot:** The file attached to this target looks much juicier than expected.
☎️ **Police Trace Call:** You hear a faint click on the line. Bad sign.
☎️ **Script Fumble:** You lose your place in the script and have to recover mid-lie.

approach.text: It’s late. The clerk is half-asleep, doom-scrolling behind the counter.
Two customers are inside. One keeps glancing at the counter like they’re nosy.
Rain’s hammering down. Your hoodie is already soaked and heavy.
A delivery truck is blocking half the storefront. Perfect cover… or perfect trap.
You notice a little camera sticker on the door. Might be real, might be bait.
The clerk is on a call, laughing too loud. They’re distracted—but also unpredictable.
A bored security guard is outside… but they look like they’re about to wander off.
A group of kids are hanging near the entrance, messing around.

choices.label: Shoo them off with a scene

approach.text: The store just opened. Fresh register… and a fresh memory of your face.

choices.label: Act casual, grab a drink first

approach.text: Your nerves are screaming. One wrong move and you’ll look guilty without even doing anything.
You swear you recognize someone near the checkout. That’s… not ideal.

choices.label: Create a distraction to split attention

approach.text: The lights flicker. Could be nothing—could be a perfect moment of confusion.

method.text: You’re close enough to the till to make a move.
The clerk’s hands are shaking already. They look like they’ll panic fast.
You hear the register ping like it’s been opened recently.

choices.label: Act like you’re paying, then grab

method.text: A customer walks in mid-move. Bad timing.
The clerk’s hand twitches toward something under the counter.
The cash drawer sticks. It’s not sliding out cleanly.

choices.label: Take what you can and go

method.text: The clerk talks tough like they’re not scared. Either bravado… or they’ve got help nearby.
You catch your reflection in a convex mirror. Cameras might have a perfect angle right now.
The clerk drops a set of keys and fumbles to pick them up.
A side door chime rings. Someone might be coming from the back room.
The till is heavy on coins and small notes—annoying to carry, easy to spill.
The clerk blurts ‘manager!’ toward the back room.

greed.text: You’ve got a moment. Do you just take the till and bounce?
The clerk hesitates at the safe keypad. Every second feels loud.
You hear movement in the back room. Someone might be coming out.
There’s a wallet sitting on the counter. Probably a customer’s.
A tip jar sits right in reach. Petty… but easy.
The clerk pleads quietly: ‘Please… just take it and go.’

choices.label: Sweep shelves on the way out

greed.text: The register is open. The longer it stays open, the more obvious this gets.
You see a silhouette outside the window peeking in.
The safe has a time delay. Not long… but long enough to feel like forever.
The clerk offers to hand over a bundle from under the counter if you leave now.
There’s a CCTV monitor behind the counter showing multiple angles—including yours.
The clerk starts stalling with fake fumbling: ‘I can’t… it’s stuck…’

exit.text: You spot a rear door behind the counter. It’s slightly ajar.
The front door bell is loud. Leaving through it will be obvious.
A customer is right near the entrance, staring like they know something’s up.
You hear a siren in the distance. Could be unrelated… or not.
The alley has two people smoking. They look up as you approach.
You nearly trip off the curb. One more stumble and you’ll eat pavement.
There’s a gap in a back fence that leads to a quiet street.
A neighbor is watering plants and watching the storefront like it’s their hobby.
Someone across the street raises their phone. Recording? Or just texting?
A small shortcut cuts between buildings—tight, dark, and fast.
There’s a small crowd at a bus stop right where you’d normally run past.
At the corner, the pedestrian light flips red. Stopping looks normal. Running looks guilty.

aftermath.text: You duck into a side street. Your heart’s still hammering.
You pass a dumpster and a dark doorway. Easy place to swap layers.
A public restroom is open. Cameras might be there… but so is a sink.
You could call a ride and vanish… but phones leave trails.
You’re tempted to check social media—see if anyone posted about it.
A meetup spot is nearby. It’s safe… unless someone followed.
Your bag strap feels weak. If it snaps, you’ll leave a trail.
You catch your face in a shop window reflection. Too identifiable?
You can go home… or you can go somewhere anonymous until things cool down.
You notice receipts and packaging mixed in with the loot. That’s traceable junk.
You force yourself to breathe slower. Panic makes mistakes.
You hear footsteps behind you. Might be nothing. Might be everything.

choices.label: Duck into a shop to blend

1: A hairline crack appears in reality… then pretends it was never there.
The air tastes like static. Something is watching… patiently.
A whisper curls through the channel and dies mid‑word.
You blink — and the space between seconds feels… wider.
A faint ripple. A distant laugh. A Rift that *almost* isn’t.
2: A tear opens, stitched together by unseen hands.
Your shadow moves a fraction late. The Rift approves.
A cold pulse thumps once. The world pretends it didn’t.
Echo is near. Not close. Near.
3: The void leans in like it has a secret.
A Rift yawns open — and something inside exhales.
Reality buckles. Somewhere, a bell rings underwater.
The air goes heavy. Echo’s attention is… focused.
A thin scream of starlight slices the room, then settles.
4: The Rift opens like a wound that remembers your name.
Space fractures. Time blinks. Echo does not.
A pressure builds behind your eyes. The Rift is *hungry*.
The channel feels smaller. Like the universe stepped closer.
A silent thunder rolls through nothingness. Echo is listening.

CLAIMED_BY_OTHER_LINES: Too slow — the Rift has already taken someone.
The Rift seals behind another. You’re left with the echo of it.
Denied. Someone else stepped in first.
You reach out… and hit glass. The Rift is claimed.
EXPIRED_LINES: The Rift collapses with a sigh. Nothing remains but embarrassment.
Too late. The crack seals itself like it was never there.
The void closes. Echo is… disappointed.
Silence. The Rift is gone. Your courage arrived late.
NOTHING_LINES: You shout loud… but there’s no Echo.
The Rift stares back… then closes.
A cold breeze. A blank stare. Nothing.
No reward. No punishment. Just… judgement.
Echo listens. Then decides you weren’t worth the effort.
The void yawns… and you’re not invited.
BLESSING_LINES: A warm pulse floods your chest. **+{reward}**.
The Rift purrs. Coins fall like rain: **{reward}**.
You step out richer — and smug about it. **{reward}**.
A laugh echoes from nowhere. Your wallet feels heavier: **{reward}**.
The universe blinks. You’re rewarded for being reckless: **{reward}**.
CURSE_LINES: Your actions have angered Echo and therefore you {curse}.
A cold hand closes around your fate. You {curse}.
BLOOD_TAX_REJECT_LINES: You return with unpaid tribute. The Rift refuses you.
Echo remembers debts. You do not enter.
You try to bargain while owing blood tax? Cute.
The Rift laughs. You’re not welcome until you pay what you owe.
BLOOD_TAX_PENALTY_LINES: Echo adds an extra fee for your audacity.
Echo doubles down on your stupidity. Additional tribute demanded.
Trying to slip past a god with a debt? That costs extra.
Echo charges you for wasting its time.
CHOSEN_DENY_LINES: You’ve proven your worth. Echo asks that you rest.
Chosen already. The Rift will not spoil you twice.
Echo has marked you. Don’t get greedy.
The Rift recoils — you’re already wearing Echo’s favour.
No. Not today. Echo doesn’t reward gluttony.
Echo’s Chosen do not gamble with the Rift.
ACTIVE_CURSE_DENY_LINES: You’re still paying for your last mistake. The Rift ignores you.
Echo hasn’t finished with you yet.
The chains are still warm. Come back later.
Your curse lingers. The Rift refuses to stack your suffering.
Denied. Echo is already collecting from you.

easy: A tired nurse flags you down after a long shift.

A masked rider taps on the window and asks if you take cash only.

routeIntros: They mumble directions fast and expect you to keep up.
You glance in the mirror as they start barking out turns.
The passenger points ahead and rattles off a route from memory.
They lean forward between the seats and give you a rapid set of turns.
declineLines: You shake your head and the fare moves on to the next cab.
You pass on the fare and keep the meter idle for another minute.
You decide it is not worth the trouble and drive past.
You decline the job and wait for a better fare.

easy: A clean, quiet fare. Exactly the kind of ride you want on shift.
Easy money. No drama, no mess, no nonsense.
The passenger thanks you, pays up, and heads inside.
vip: A premium fare with premium money attached.
Smooth driving. The kind of ride that keeps your stars up.
They barely say thanks, but the payout does the talking.
sketchy: For once, the weird fare actually pays like promised.
They slide out of the cab and toss the money in without argument.
The ride felt cursed, but the cash at least looked real.

wrongTurnLines: You miss the turn and the passenger blows up, storms out, and refuses to pay.
Wrong street. Wrong mood. The fare bails on the ride and you get nothing.
One bad turn and the passenger is done with you. No payout.

noPay: At the destination they shrug, laugh, and walk off without paying.
The rider disappears the moment the door opens. Not a dollar left behind.
pukeFee: They throw up all over the back seat. You eat a nasty cleaning bill.
The passenger paints the interior. The cab is a biohazard until you clean it.
reducedShift: They spew across the floor mats. The smell kills your tips for the rest of the shift.
The rider leaves your taxi rancid. Every fare after this feels lighter on the wallet.
robbery: Before they leave, they snatch your last fare's cash and bolt into the dark.
The rider shoves your arm aside, grabs your recent earnings, and runs.
runAway: They slam the door and sprint off down a side lane before you can react.
You barely stop before they are out and gone. Fare skipped.
normal: Somehow, the sketchy fare actually pays you properly.
Against all odds, they count out the cash and leave without trouble.

tip: The passenger leaves a heavy tip for keeping it smooth.
A crisp extra note lands on the centre console. Nice.
double: They liked the service so much they doubled the fare on the spot.
VIP treatment recognised. They pay far above the meter.
escape: Halfway through, they tell you to lose a tail and sweeten the deal if you do.
A black SUV lingers a little too long behind you. Your passenger offers more to keep moving.

edge: {a} wants a seat on an edge.
{a} needs one of the end seats.
notEdge: {a} refuses to sit on an edge.

distance: {a} sits exactly two seats away from {b}.

success: The table settles into place. Echo lets the room breathe.

failure: The room collapses back into argument.
The seating chart tears itself into useless little strips.
wrong: A few chairs scrape in protest.

notEdge: {a} cannot sit where the circle breaks.

The circle breaks, and Echo gives nothing more.
